1	import { afterAll, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, mock, spyOn } from "bun:test"
2	import type { ToolContext } from "@opencode-ai/plugin/tool"
3	import * as fs from "node:fs"
4	import { createSkillTool } from "./tools"
5	import { SkillMcpManager } from "../../features/skill-mcp-manager"
6	import type { LoadedSkill } from "../../features/opencode-skill-loader/types"
7	import type { CommandInfo } from "../slashcommand/types"
8	import type { Tool as McpTool } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js"
9	
10	const originalReadFileSync = fs.readFileSync.bind(fs)
11	
12	mock.module("node:fs", () => ({
13	  ...fs,
14	  readFileSync: (path: string, encoding?: string) => {
15	    if (typeof path === "string" && path.includes("/skills/")) {
16	      return `---
17	description: Test skill description
18	---
19	Test skill body content`
20	    }
21	    return originalReadFileSync(path, encoding as BufferEncoding)
22	  },
23	}))
24	
25	afterAll(() => {
26	  mock.restore()
27	})
28	
29	function createMockSkill(name: string, options: { agent?: string } = {}): LoadedSkill {
30	  return {
31	    name,
32	    path: `/test/skills/${name}/SKILL.md`,
33	    resolvedPath: `/test/skills/${name}`,
34	    definition: {
35	      name,
36	      description: `Test skill ${name}`,
37	      template: "Test template",
38	      agent: options.agent,
39	    },
40	    scope: "opencode-project",
41	  }
42	}
43	
44	function createMockSkillWithMcp(name: string, mcpServers: Record<string, unknown>): LoadedSkill {
45	  return {
46	    name,
47	    path: `/test/skills/${name}/SKILL.md`,
48	    resolvedPath: `/test/skills/${name}`,
49	    definition: {
50	      name,
51	      description: `Test skill ${name}`,
52	      template: "Test template",
53	    },
54	    scope: "opencode-project",
55	    mcpConfig: mcpServers as LoadedSkill["mcpConfig"],
56	  }
57	}
58	
59	const mockContext: ToolContext = {
60	  sessionID: "test-session",
61	  messageID: "msg-1",
62	  agent: "test-agent",
63	  directory: "/test",
64	  worktree: "/test",
65	  abort: new AbortController().signal,
66	  metadata: () => {},
67	  ask: async () => {},
68	}
69	
70	describe("skill tool - synchronous description", () => {
71	  it("includes available_items immediately when skills are pre-provided", () => {
72	    // given
73	    const loadedSkills = [createMockSkill("test-skill")]
74	
75	    // when
76	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
77	
78	    // then
79	    expect(tool.description).toContain("<available_items>")
80	    expect(tool.description).toContain("test-skill")
81	  })
82	
83	  it("includes all pre-provided skills in available_items immediately", () => {
84	    // given
85	    const loadedSkills = [
86	      createMockSkill("playwright"),
87	      createMockSkill("frontend-ui-ux"),
88	      createMockSkill("git-master"),
89	    ]
90	
91	    // when
92	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
93	
94	    // then
95	    expect(tool.description).toContain("<available_items>")
96	    expect(tool.description).toContain("playwright")
97	    expect(tool.description).toContain("frontend-ui-ux")
98	    expect(tool.description).toContain("git-master")
99	  })
100	
101	  it("shows no-skills message immediately when empty skills are pre-provided", () => {
102	    // given / #when
103	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: [] })
104	
105	    // then
106	    expect(tool.description).toContain("No skills are currently available")
107	  })
108	})
109	
110	describe("skill tool - agent restriction", () => {
111	  it("allows skill without agent restriction to any agent", async () => {
112	    // given
113	    const loadedSkills = [createMockSkill("public-skill")]
114	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
115	    const context = { ...mockContext, agent: "any-agent" }
116	
117	    // when
118	    const result = await tool.execute({ name: "public-skill" }, context)
119	
120	    // then
121	    expect(result).toContain("public-skill")
122	  })
123	
124	  it("allows skill when agent matches restriction", async () => {
125	    // given
126	    const loadedSkills = [createMockSkill("restricted-skill", { agent: "sisyphus" })]
127	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
128	    const context = { ...mockContext, agent: "sisyphus" }
129	
130	    // when
131	    const result = await tool.execute({ name: "restricted-skill" }, context)
132	
133	    // then
134	    expect(result).toContain("restricted-skill")
135	  })
136	
137	  it("throws error when agent does not match restriction", async () => {
138	    // given
139	    const loadedSkills = [createMockSkill("sisyphus-only-skill", { agent: "sisyphus" })]
140	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
141	    const context = { ...mockContext, agent: "oracle" }
142	
143	    // when / #then
144	    await expect(tool.execute({ name: "sisyphus-only-skill" }, context)).rejects.toThrow(
145	      'Skill "sisyphus-only-skill" is restricted to agent "sisyphus"'
146	    )
147	  })
148	
149	  it("throws error when context agent is undefined for restricted skill", async () => {
150	    // given
151	    const loadedSkills = [createMockSkill("sisyphus-only-skill", { agent: "sisyphus" })]
152	    const tool = createSkillTool({ skills: loadedSkills })
153	    const contextWithoutAgent = { ...mockContext, agent: undefined as unknown as string }
154	
155	    // when / #then
156	    await expect(tool.execute({ name: "sisyphus-only-skill" }, contextWithoutAgent)).rejects.toThrow(
157	      'Skill "sisyphus-only-skill" is restricted to agent "sisyphus"'
158	    )
159	  })
160	
161	})
162	
163	describe("skill tool - MCP schema display", () => {
164	  let manager: SkillMcpManager
165	  let loadedSkills: LoadedSkill[]
166	  let sessionID: string
167	
168	  beforeEach(() => {
169	    manager = new SkillMcpManager()
170	    loadedSkills = []
171	    sessionID = "test-session-1"
172	  })
173	
174	  describe("formatMcpCapabilities with inputSchema", () => {
175	    it("displays tool inputSchema when available", async () => {
176	      // given
177	      const mockToolsWithSchema: McpTool[] = [
178	        {
179	          name: "browser_type",
180	          description: "Type text into an element",
181	          inputSchema: {
182	            type: "object",
183	            properties: {
184	              element: { type: "string", description: "Human-readable element description" },
185	              ref: { type: "string", description: "Element reference from page snapshot" },
186	              text: { type: "string", description: "Text to type into the element" },
187	              submit: { type: "boolean", description: "Submit form after typing" },
188	            },
189	            required: ["element", "ref", "text"],
190	          },
191	        },
192	      ]
193	
194	      loadedSkills = [
195	        createMockSkillWithMcp("test-skill", {
196	          playwright: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@anthropic-ai/mcp-playwright"] },
197	        }),
198	      ]
199	
200	      // Mock manager.listTools to return our mock tools
201	      spyOn(manager, "listTools").mockResolvedValue(mockToolsWithSchema)
202	      spyOn(manager, "listResources").mockResolvedValue([])
203	      spyOn(manager, "listPrompts").mockResolvedValue([])
204	
205	      const tool = createSkillTool({
206	        skills: loadedSkills,
207	        mcpManager: manager,
208	        getSessionID: () => sessionID,
209	      })
210	
211	      // when
212	      const result = await tool.execute({ name: "test-skill" }, mockContext)
213	
214	      // then
215	      // Should include inputSchema details
216	      expect(result).toContain("browser_type")
217	      expect(result).toContain("inputSchema")
218	      expect(result).toContain("element")
219	      expect(result).toContain("ref")
220	      expect(result).toContain("text")
221	      expect(result).toContain("submit")
222	      expect(result).toContain("required")
223	    })
224	
225	    it("displays multiple tools with their schemas", async () => {
226	      // given
227	      const mockToolsWithSchema: McpTool[] = [
228	        {
229	          name: "browser_navigate",
230	          description: "Navigate to a URL",
231	          inputSchema: {
232	            type: "object",
233	            properties: {
234	              url: { type: "string", description: "URL to navigate to" },
235	            },
236	            required: ["url"],
237	          },
238	        },
239	        {
240	          name: "browser_click",
241	          description: "Click an element",
242	          inputSchema: {
243	            type: "object",
244	            properties: {
245	              element: { type: "string" },
246	              ref: { type: "string" },
247	            },
248	            required: ["element", "ref"],
249	          },
250	        },
251	      ]
252	
253	      loadedSkills = [
254	        createMockSkillWithMcp("playwright-skill", {
255	          playwright: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@anthropic-ai/mcp-playwright"] },
256	        }),
257	      ]
258	
259	      spyOn(manager, "listTools").mockResolvedValue(mockToolsWithSchema)
260	      spyOn(manager, "listResources").mockResolvedValue([])
261	      spyOn(manager, "listPrompts").mockResolvedValue([])
262	
263	      const tool = createSkillTool({
264	        skills: loadedSkills,
265	        mcpManager: manager,
266	        getSessionID: () => sessionID,
267	      })
268	
269	      // when
270	      const result = await tool.execute({ name: "playwright-skill" }, mockContext)
271	
272	      // then
273	      expect(result).toContain("browser_navigate")
274	      expect(result).toContain("browser_click")
275	      expect(result).toContain("url")
276	      expect(result).toContain("Navigate to a URL")
277	    })
278	
279	    it("handles tools without inputSchema gracefully", async () => {
280	      // given
281	      const mockToolsMinimal: McpTool[] = [
282	        {
283	          name: "simple_tool",
284	          inputSchema: { type: "object" },
285	        },
286	      ]
287	
288	      loadedSkills = [
289	        createMockSkillWithMcp("simple-skill", {
290	          simple: { command: "echo", args: ["test"] },
291	        }),
292	      ]
293	
294	      spyOn(manager, "listTools").mockResolvedValue(mockToolsMinimal)
295	      spyOn(manager, "listResources").mockResolvedValue([])
296	      spyOn(manager, "listPrompts").mockResolvedValue([])
297	
298	      const tool = createSkillTool({
299	        skills: loadedSkills,
300	        mcpManager: manager,
301	        getSessionID: () => sessionID,
302	      })

533	    
534	    // Get initial description - it will build from empty or disk skills

536	    
537	    // when: execute() is called, which clears cache AND gets fresh skills
538	    // Note: In real scenario, execute() would discover new skills from disk
539	    // For testing, we verify the mechanism: execute() should invalidate cachedDescription

543	    try {

content.text:
Now I can see the issue clearly. Let me look at how skills are resolved. The `execute` function in `tools.ts` does exact matching by name (`skills.find(s => s.name.toLowerCase() === requestedName.toLowerCase())`), but the skill names stored are fully qualified (like `superpowers/systematic-debugging`), while the description shows them as just the short name (like `systematic-debugging`).

Wait, let me re-read the description format. In `formatCombinedDescription`, the skill is shown as `/<skill.name>`. If `skill.name` is `superpowers/systematic-debugging`, it would show as `/superpowers/systematic-debugging`, not just `systematic-debugging`. Let me check how the skills are actually loaded and what names they
